playbook.intent: When the agent ships a hallucinated answer, mis-calls a tool, or jailbreaks itself, the team produces a structured postmortem that attributes cause to model/prompt/tool/context layer, lands a regression eval, and prevents recurrence.
playbook.scope: When the agent ships a hallucinated answer, mis-calls a tool, or jailbreaks itself, the team produces a structured postmortem that attributes cause to model/prompt/tool/context layer, lands a regression eval, and prevents recurrence.

success-criteria.criterion: Frame stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note
Discover stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note
Design stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note
Build stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note
Validate stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note
Close stage complete with written artifact and decision-gate note

stage.name: Frame
stage.intent: State the outcome, constraints, and exit-condition in one paragraph.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 1 (Frame) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 1 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

stage.name: Discover
stage.intent: Pull evidence and prior art before any design decision.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 2 (Discover) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 2 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

stage.name: Design
stage.intent: Sketch target structure with explicit cuts and trade-offs.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 3 (Design) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 3 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

stage.name: Build
stage.intent: Implement the smallest version that proves the chain works.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 4 (Build) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 4 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

stage.name: Validate
stage.intent: Test against the rubric or real data before broad rollout.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 5 (Validate) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 5 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

stage.name: Close
stage.intent: Apply decision gate, document outcome, archive for re-use.

tasks.task: Read the assigned methodology references and reconcile them with this stage's goal.
Produce the stage artifact named in 'outputs' and store it where the next stage can read it.
Re-check the decision gate before advancing; loop within this stage if it fails.

outputs.output: Written artifact for stage 6 (Close) named per the project's docs convention
Decision-gate note (advance / iterate / kill) appended to the playbook log

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stage 6 artifact exists in writing AND the methodology chain assigned to this stage has been applied. Iterate or escalate otherwise.

extra.value: [Skipping decision-gate verification collapses the playbook into a checklist, Treating outputs as optional kills traceability and future re-use, Running stages out of order because one feels more urgent absorbs risk downstream]

['Did every stage produce a written artifact a stranger could read?', 'Did I advance only when the decision-gate criterion was met in writing?', 'Did I document every methodology gap before it shipped to a user?']

{human: faion get-content run-a-blameless-agent-incident-postmortem-when-the-agent-is-the-defendant --format md, agent: faion get-content run-a-blameless-agent-incident-postmortem-when-the-agent-is-the-defendant --format context, json: faion get-content run-a-blameless-agent-incident-postmortem-when-the-agent-is-the-defendant --format json}
[{version: 1.0.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from F-059-063 chunk-03; persona=P7, angle=synthesis'}]
